commands.review_summary:
The branch mirrors validated writable Docker bind host roots into Codex workspace-write policy, disables native Code Mode for unrepresentable Docker bind layouts, exposes sandbox_exec/process fallbacks, and adds regression tests plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main sends only the effective cwd as Codex writableRoots and hides Docker sandbox_exec, while docs say bind write modes are independent of workspaceAccess. I did not run a local Docker repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair lane is needed because the code blocker appears resolved; remaining action is maintainer sandbox/security boundary review and normal PR landing decision.

target.title: fix(codex): honor Docker bind write policy
target.body:
Summary:
- mirror writable Docker bind host roots into Codex app-server workspace-write policy when the bind can be represented safely on the host
- disable Codex native Code Mode for Docker bind aliases such as `host:/data:rw`, and for writable bind roots that contain read-only child bind shadows
- expose OpenClaw `sandbox_exec` / `sandbox_process` as the fallback shell surface when Docker bind paths cannot be represented by Codex host-native Code Mode
- add focused regression coverage for writable bind roots, alias detection, read-only shadow detection, Codex native tool gating, and Docker fallback tool exposure

Fixes #83737

Verification:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` (180 tests passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sandbox/fs-paths.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` (3 files, 204 tests passed before the fallback follow-up)
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean, no accepted/actionable findings)
- AWS Crabbox `run_4f418fe2f309`, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_1164647677d0`, exit 0

Real behavior proof:
Behavior addressed: Docker sandbox with `workspaceAccess: "ro"` and external writable bind `host:/data:rw` must leave the model with a working sandbox shell/file mutation path when Codex host-native Code Mode is disabled because it cannot represent container-only paths.
Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox direct provider, `run_4f418fe2f309`, lease `cbx_1164647677d0`, Linux target with Node 22, pnpm, Docker 29, built OpenClaw output, and a locally built `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim` image.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Synced the patched checkout to AWS, installed Node/pnpm/Docker, ran `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, `pnpm build`, and `scripts/sandbox-setup.sh`; then created a real Docker sandbox using `workspaceAccess: "ro"`, `${bindRoot}:/data:rw`, and `dangerouslyAllowExternalBindSources: true`, ran `runCodexAppServerAttempt` against a protocol-shaped fake app-server client, inspected the actual `thread/start` payload, invoked the exposed `sandbox_exec` dynamic tool through the app-server `item/tool/call` request handler with `printf patched-from-sandbox >/data/proof.txt && cat /data/proof.txt`, and read the host bind file.
Evidence after fix: Proof output reported `thread/start features.code_mode false`, `thread/start dynamicTools sessions_list,sessions_history,sessions_send,sessions_spawn,sessions_yield,subagents,session_status,sandbox_exec,sandbox_process`, `thread/start environments []`, `sandbox_exec success true`, `sandbox_exec output [{"type":"inputText","text":"patched-from-sandbox"}]`, and `host bind proof patched-from-sandbox`.
Observed result after fix: Codex native Code Mode was disabled for the Docker container-path alias, the OpenClaw sandbox shell fallback remained available, and the fallback wrote `/data/proof.txt` through the Docker bind so the host saw `patched-from-sandbox`.
What was not tested: A real OpenAI-authenticated Codex model turn was not run; the proof used a protocol-shaped fake app-server client so no live credentials were required while still exercising the real OpenClaw app-server setup and dynamic tool call path.
